name: queries
context: Select, insert, update, delete with Drizzle's type-safe SQL-like query builder and relational query API
info: Drizzle provides two query APIs: the SQL-like query builder (db.select().from().where()) that maps 1:1 to SQL with full type inference, and the relational query API (db.query.*.findMany/findFirst) for nested data with `with` clauses. The builder supports all standard SQL operations: select (partial, distinct, computed columns), insert (single, batch, upsert, returning), update (set, where, from, returning), delete (where, returning), joins (inner, left, right, full), subqueries, CTEs (WITH), aggregations (count, sum, avg, min, max, groupBy, having), and raw SQL via the sql template tag. Filter operators: eq, ne, gt, lt, gte, lte, like, ilike, inArray, notInArray, isNull, isNotNull, between, and, or, not. Pagination via limit/offset or cursor-based. Prepared statements for performance. See [[drizzle/schema]] for table definitions and [[drizzle/relations]] for enabling the relational API.

sections.title: Select queries
sections.body: `db.select().from(table)` returns all rows. Pass a column map for partial select: `db.select({ id: users.id }).from(users)`. Chain `.where()`, `.orderBy()`, `.limit()`, `.offset()`. Use `selectDistinct()` for DISTINCT. Computed columns via `sql<type>\`expression\``. See [[drizzle/schema]] for table definitions.

sections.title: Insert operations
sections.body: `db.insert(table).values(data)` inserts one row, `.values([...])` inserts many. Chain `.returning()` (PostgreSQL/SQLite) to get inserted rows. Upsert with `.onConflictDoUpdate({ target, set })` or `.onConflictDoNothing()`. MySQL uses `.onDuplicateKeyUpdate({ set })`. Type safety via `typeof table.$inferInsert`.

sections.title: Update and delete
sections.body: `db.update(table).set(values).where(condition)` updates rows. `db.delete(table).where(condition)` deletes rows. Both support `.returning()` on PostgreSQL/SQLite. Pass `null` to set a column to NULL, `undefined` is ignored. Use `sql\`NOW()\`` for SQL expressions in set values.

sections.title: Joins
sections.body: Chain `.innerJoin(table, condition)`, `.leftJoin()`, `.rightJoin()`, `.fullJoin()` after `.from()`. The condition is typically `eq(table1.col, table2.col)`. Select specific columns from joined tables in the select map. For complex joins, use the sql template tag. For nested data loading, prefer the relational query API via [[drizzle/relations]].

sections.title: Aggregations
sections.body: Import `count`, `sum`, `avg`, `min`, `max` from `drizzle-orm`. Use in select: `db.select({ total: count() }).from(table)`. Chain `.groupBy(column)` and `.having(condition)` for grouped aggregations. The `db.$count(table, filter?)` helper returns a plain number. For complex aggregations, use `sql<number>\`expression\``.

sections.title: Relational query API
sections.body: `db.query.tableName.findMany({ with: { relation: true } })` loads nested related data. Requires relations defined via [[drizzle/relations]] and schema passed to `drizzle()`. Supports nested `with`, `where`, `columns`, `limit`, `orderBy` at each level. `findFirst` returns one result. This replaces manual JOIN construction for common nested data patterns.

sections.title: Transactions
sections.body: `db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })` wraps operations in a transaction. Use `tx` instead of `db` inside the callback. Nested `tx.transaction()` creates savepoints. If the callback throws, the transaction is rolled back. All CRUD operations work on the transaction object.

sections.title: Raw SQL and prepared statements
sections.body: The `sql` template tag from `drizzle-orm` allows raw SQL in where clauses, computed columns, and full queries. `sql<Type>\`expression\`` provides type inference. `db.execute(sql\`...\`)` runs raw queries. Prepared statements via `.prepare('name')` + `.execute(params)` with `placeholder('name')` for parameters — improves performance for repeated queries.

links.context: Table definitions used in from() and insert()
Define relations to enable the relational query API

related.note: Tables must be defined before they can be queried
Relations enable the db.query.*.findMany API with nested with clauses
Prisma uses an object-based query API; Drizzle uses SQL-like chaining

scripts.name: Drizzle Query Examples
scripts.description: Run common Drizzle ORM query patterns: CRUD, joins, aggregations, transactions

depends.reason: Tables must be defined to query them
Relations are needed for the relational query API (db.query)
